title: check err returned by newGenericDecompressor
body:
Hello!

I used SAST tool Svace and found a couple of newGenericDecompressor function usages, where returned possibly non-nil `err` is not checked before dereferencing returned decompressor. It may lead to nil ptr dereferencing.

This PR adds check for `err` to prevent dereferencing potentially nullable decompressor.

Found by Linux Verification Center (linuxtesting.org) with SVACE

comments.body: LGTM, honestly, took me a minute to get it before it was a facepalm
I've added the no-test label and restarted tests.

The return should probably be changed to return nil instead of err, given err is now guaranteed to be nil... but that's not worth re-pushing over.
/lgtm
/approve
